blocks.content: Two cups of coffee every morning. Always two. He made hers the way she liked it — a little more than was reasonable — and she drank it standing at the kitchen window looking at the yard, and that was the whole thing, that was everything: her at the window with her coffee, the yard going through its seasons outside the glass, the morning happening quietly around her.
Christmas trees. Always too tall. She was always right and he was always wrong and neither of them ever stopped arguing about it and neither of them ever actually wanted to.
Maya and Nathan got married on a Saturday in June. Cassandra cried through the entire ceremony and Maya told her she was embarrassing herself and then Maya cried too.
Lena ran the gallery now. She still called Cassandra from work — habit, affection, the best kind of both.

Thomas and Claire Hailey grew older. Slower. Still present at every table that mattered. Thomas still shook Ethan's hand and held it a moment longer than a handshake required, because some things didn't change.
She had dyed her hair sometime in the middle years — a warm brown she had kept ever since. He had drawn her in both.
The house was the same but fuller — more lived in, more marked with the specific evidence of two people who had been in it for years and had made it entirely their own.

The ordinary life she had asked for in a hospital room years ago — the Christmas tree, the Sunday cooking, the farmers market on Saturdays, all the unremarkable pieces of a life shared completely — they had gotten it.
